title: Research Associate (Singapore Literature)
description:
The English Programme at Nanyang Technological University hosts a dynamic community of students and professors engaged in a wide variety of literary passions and projects. It is a research-intensive department with a particular focus in five key research areas: Singapore Literature, Modern Literature, Contemporary Literature and Culture, Period Studies, and Irish Studies.

For more details, please view https://www.ntu.edu.sg/soh/about-us/english.

We are looking for a Research Associate to provide research and administrative support for an MOE Tier 1-funded project, “Smart Nation: A Literary Atlas”. The project is a scholarly intervention that sets out to provide a fresh perspective on the complex negotiations between state, society, and science and technology in Singapore in order to address the vital and urgent questions pertaining to the necessary re-evaluation of these relationships in the twenty-first century and the role that culture plays in this task. Key outputs include a monograph on cultural representations of science and technology in Singaporean literature and a seminar series featuring prominent researchers working at the intersection of science and technology and literature.

Key Responsibilities:

  • Collate annotated bibliography of Singaporean literary works that engage with science and technology as well as related themes

  • Close read Singaporean literary works that engage with science and technology as well as related themes

  • Assist with archival research, both online and on-site

  • Assist with the preparation of manuscripts

  • Assist with administrative tasks pertaining to the organization of a seminar series

  • Oversee a small team of undergraduate student research assistants

Job Requirements:

  • Master qualification degree in English Literature or related field

  • Good written and oral communication skills

  • Ability to work independently under strict timelines with an eye for detail

  • Familiarity with Singaporean literature and/or literature engaging with science and technology-related issues is an advantage.

  • Entry level candidates are welcome to apply.

We regret to inform that only shortlisted candidates will be notified.

postedCompany.description:
A research-intensive public university, Nanyang Technological University, Singapore (NTU Singapore) has 33,000 undergraduate and postgraduate students in the Engineering, Business, Science, Humanities, Arts, & Social Sciences, and Graduate colleges. It also has a medical school, the Lee Kong Chian School of Medicine, established jointly with Imperial College London.


NTU is also home to world-class autonomous institutes – the National Institute of Education, S Rajaratnam School of International Studies, Earth Observatory of Singapore, and Singapore Centre for Environmental Life Sciences Engineering – and various leading research centres such as the Nanyang Environment & Water Research Institute (NEWRI) and Energy Research Institute @ NTU (ERI@N).


Ranked amongst the world’s top universities by QS, NTU has also been named the world’s top young university for the past seven years. The University’s main campus is frequently listed among the Top 15 most beautiful university campuses in the world and has 57 Green Mark-certified (equivalent to LEED-certified) buildings, of which 95% are certified Green Mark Platinum. Apart from its main campus, NTU also has a campus in Novena, Singapore’s healthcare district.


Under the NTU Smart Campus vision, the University harnesses the power of digital technology and tech-enabled solutions to support better learning and living experiences, the discovery of new knowledge, and the sustainability of resources.


For more information, visit www.ntu.edu.sg
postedCompany.name: NANYANG TECHNOLOGICAL UNIVERSITY
